source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in figure (a), there are two small sliders A and B, each with a mass of $m$, on a rough, insulated horizontal plane. Their charges are $q (q>0)$ and $-q$, respectively. A light, smooth, insulated rod and a light, insulated spring are fixed at the right end of A, with the spring initially at its natural length. A uniform electric field with a magnitude of $E$ is present throughout the space, directed horizontally to the right. The maximum static friction between A, B and the horizontal plane is equal to the sliding friction, both with a magnitude of $2qE$. At $t=0$, A moves to the right with an initial velocity of $v_0$, while B is at rest. At time $t_1$, A reaches position S with a velocity of $v_1$, and the spring is not yet in contact with B. At time $t_2$, the velocity of A reaches its maximum, and the magnitude of the spring force at this time is $3qE$. At the instant before the rod collides with B, the velocity of A is $2v_1$, which occurs at $t=t_3$. The $v-t$ graph of A in the time interval $0~t_3$ is shown in figure (b), where $v_1$ is the minimum speed in the graph, and $t_1$, $t_2$, and $t_3$ are all unknowns. During the motion, A and B are on the same straight line, and the charges of A and B remain constant. The Coulomb force between them is equivalent to the electrostatic force between point charges in a vacuum, with the electrostatic constant being $k$. There is no mechanical energy loss the instant B contacts the spring, and the spring remains within its elastic limit at all times.

1. Determine the work done by the net external force on A during the time interval $0~t_1$.
2. Determine the distance between A and B at time $t_1$.
3. Calculate the total work done by the uniform electric field on A and B during the time interval $t_1~t_2$.
4. If the initial velocity of A is increased such that its velocity at position S is $2v_1$, determine the velocity of A at the instant before the rod collides with B.

issues: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to figure (a) and figure (b) and a v-t graph
some quantities and events are defined via the figures (position S, graph minimum, t1/t2/t3)
multi_step: reasoning spans mechanics, friction, electric field, Coulomb interaction, spring force, and graph interpretation
the setup uses multiple time points (t1, t2, t3) and conditional events before contact/collision
final part asks for a modified initial condition and resulting pre-collision speed, indicating chained inference
verifiability: answer_present=true with four explicit symbolic results
answer_type is short text/math expressions rather than open-ended explanation
results are concise and in principle checkable against derived symbolic expressions, though image-linked setup makes full automatic verification less trivial

risk_flags: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.
figure-dependent definitions may complicate exact automatic verification

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, primary image, and answer are all available.
Initial scoring is strong, with very high image dependency and multi-step reasoning value.
The existing image file appears valid, so the image_paths inconsistency is a metadata issue rather than a missing-core-asset issue.
Although figure-dependent setup and multi-part symbolic answers may make verification harder, the sample remains recoverable and high-value.

At $t=0$, A moves to the right with an initial velocity of $v_0$, while B is at rest.
At time $t_1$, A reaches position S with a velocity of $v_1$, and the spring is not yet in contact with B.
At time $t_2$, the velocity of A reaches its maximum, and the magnitude of the spring force at this time is $3qE$.
The $v$-$t$ graph of A in the time interval $0\sim t_3$ is shown in figure (b), where $v_1$ is the minimum speed in the graph, and $t_1$, $t_2$, and $t_3$ are all unknowns.

node_records.canonical_value:
As shown in figure (a), there are two small sliders A and B, each with a mass of $m$, on a rough, insulated horizontal plane. Their charges are $q$ ($q>0$) and $-q$, respectively. A light, smooth, insulated rod and a light, insulated spring are fixed at the right end of A, with the spring initially at its natural length. A uniform electric field with a magnitude of $E$ is present throughout the space, directed horizontally to the right. The maximum static friction between A, B and the horizontal plane is equal to the sliding friction, both with a magnitude of $2qE$. At $t=0$, A moves to the right with an initial velocity of $v_0$, while B is at rest. At time $t_1$, A reaches position S with a velocity of $v_1$, and the spring is not yet in contact with B. At time $t_2$, the velocity of A reaches its maximum, and the magnitude of the spring force at this time is $3qE$. At the instant before the rod collides with B, the velocity of A is $2v_1$, which occurs at $t=t_3$. The $v$-$t$ graph of A in the time interval $0\sim t_3$ is shown in figure (b), where $v_1$ is the minimum speed in the graph, and $t_1$, $t_2$, and $t_3$ are all unknowns. During the motion, A and B are on the same straight line, and the charges of A and B remain constant. The Coulomb force between them is equivalent to the electrostatic force between point charges in a vacuum, with the electrostatic constant being $k$. There is no mechanical energy loss the instant B contacts the spring, and the spring remains within its elastic limit at all times.

1. Determine the work done by the net external force on A during the time interval $0\sim t_1$.
2. Determine the distance between A and B at time $t_1$.
3. Calculate the total work done by the uniform electric field on A and B during the time interval $t_1\sim t_2$.
4. If the initial velocity of A is increased such that its velocity at position S is $2v_1$, determine the velocity of A at the instant before the rod collides with B.

normalized_value.title: PhysReason 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text:
As shown in figure (a), there are two small sliders A and B, each with a mass of $m$, on a rough, insulated horizontal plane. Their charges are $q$ ($q>0$) and $-q$, respectively. A light, smooth, insulated rod and a light, insulated spring are fixed at the right end of A, with the spring initially at its natural length. A uniform electric field with a magnitude of $E$ is present throughout the space, directed horizontally to the right. The maximum static friction between A, B and the horizontal plane is equal to the sliding friction, both with a magnitude of $2qE$. At $t=0$, A moves to the right with an initial velocity of $v_0$, while B is at rest. At time $t_1$, A reaches position S with a velocity of $v_1$, and the spring is not yet in contact with B. At time $t_2$, the velocity of A reaches its maximum, and the magnitude of the spring force at this time is $3qE$. At the instant before the rod collides with B, the velocity of A is $2v_1$, which occurs at $t=t_3$. The $v$-$t$ graph of A in the time interval $0\sim t_3$ is shown in figure (b), where $v_1$ is the minimum speed in the graph, and $t_1$, $t_2$, and $t_3$ are all unknowns. During the motion, A and B are on the same straight line, and the charges of A and B remain constant. The Coulomb force between them is equivalent to the electrostatic force between point charges in a vacuum, with the electrostatic constant being $k$. There is no mechanical energy loss the instant B contacts the spring, and the spring remains within its elastic limit at all times.

1. Determine the work done by the net external force on A during the time interval $0\sim t_1$.
2. Determine the distance between A and B at time $t_1$.
3. Calculate the total work done by the uniform electric field on A and B during the time interval $t_1\sim t_2$.
4. If the initial velocity of A is increased such that its velocity at position S is $2v_1$, determine the velocity of A at the instant before the rod collides with B.

field_audit_records.before_value:
As shown in figure (a), there are two small sliders A and B, each with a mass of $m$, on a rough, insulated horizontal plane. Their charges are $q (q>0)$ and $-q$, respectively. A light, smooth, insulated rod and a light, insulated spring are fixed at the right end of A, with the spring initially at its natural length. A uniform electric field with a magnitude of $E$ is present throughout the space, directed horizontally to the right. The maximum static friction between A, B and the horizontal plane is equal to the sliding friction, both with a magnitude of $2qE$. At $t=0$, A moves to the right with an initial velocity of $v_0$, while B is at rest. At time $t_1$, A reaches position S with a velocity of $v_1$, and the spring is not yet in contact with B. At time $t_2$, the velocity of A reaches its maximum, and the magnitude of the spring force at this time is $3qE$. At the instant before the rod collides with B, the velocity of A is $2v_1$, which occurs at $t=t_3$. The $v-t$ graph of A in the time interval $0~t_3$ is shown in figure (b), where $v_1$ is the minimum speed in the graph, and $t_1$, $t_2$, and $t_3$ are all unknowns. During the motion, A and B are on the same straight line, and the charges of A and B remain constant. The Coulomb force between them is equivalent to the electrostatic force between point charges in a vacuum, with the electrostatic constant being $k$. There is no mechanical energy loss the instant B contacts the spring, and the spring remains within its elastic limit at all times.

1. Determine the work done by the net external force on A during the time interval $0~t_1$.
2. Determine the distance between A and B at time $t_1$.
3. Calculate the total work done by the uniform electric field on A and B during the time interval $t_1~t_2$.
4. If the initial velocity of A is increased such that its velocity at position S is $2v_1$, determine the velocity of A at the instant before the rod collides with B.
